9d704efc-2d3e-4706-a512-dda0ff86c988.label: text_chunk
9d704efc-2d3e-4706-a512-dda0ff86c988.text:
MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

GENERAL

The following MD&A is intended to assist the reader in understanding our financial condition and results of operations, including an evaluation of the amounts and certainty of cash flows from operations and from outside sources, and is provided as a supplement to and should be read in conjunction with the condensed consolidated financial statements and related notes in Item 1.

18f561d0-c7a8-49e4-9d99-fa38b63b05bd.label: text_chunk
18f561d0-c7a8-49e4-9d99-fa38b63b05bd.text:
Financial Statements in this Form 10-Q.References to operational variances pertain to period-over-period changes that exclude the impact of foreign exchange rates.

 Although foreign exchange rate changes are part of our business, they are not within our control and because they can mask positive or negative trends in the business, we believe presenting operational variances excluding these foreign exchange changes provides useful information to evaluate our results.

2dcc9477-9233-4381-88a4-d761ea62c010.label: text_chunk
2dcc9477-9233-4381-88a4-d761ea62c010.text:
OVERVIEW OF OUR PERFORMANCE, OPERATING ENVIRONMENT, STRATEGY AND OUTLOOK

Our Business and Strategy ––Pfizer Inc. is a research-based, global biopharmaceutical company. We apply science and our global resources to bring therapies to people that extend and significantly improve their lives.

b2f06ae3-8230-48c7-9897-47f8280f806e.label: text_chunk
b2f06ae3-8230-48c7-9897-47f8280f806e.text:
In 2023, we are making additional investments in both R&D and SI&A to support Pfizer’s near- and longer-term growth plans, including to support anticipated new launches, commercial launch of COVID-19 products, potential pipeline programs and recently acquired assets.

 We manage our commercial operations through a global structure consisting of two operating segments: Biopharma and Business Innovation. Biopharma is the only reportable segment.

a74b7f3d-a5d6-47df-b913-3acb7e5c4ed2.label: text_chunk
a74b7f3d-a5d6-47df-b913-3acb7e5c4ed2.text: See Note 13A .We expect to incur costs of approximately $700 million in connection with separating Upjohn, of which approximately 90% has been incurred since inception and through the second quarter of 2023. These charges include costs and expenses related to separation of legal entities and transaction costs.

3062bb3a-fb47-43ec-984d-9cd6c10ab9ea.label: text_chunk
3062bb3a-fb47-43ec-984d-9cd6c10ab9ea.text: In the fourth quarter of 2022, we began taking steps through our Transforming to a More Focused Company restructuring program to optimize our end-to-end R&D operations to reduce costs and cycle times as well as to further prioritize our internal R&D portfolio in areas where our capabilities are differentiated while increasing external innovation efforts to leverage an expanding and productive biotech sector. See Note 3 .

db41600d-e0b8-4434-9f88-ab0426bce3ee.label: text_chunk
db41600d-e0b8-4434-9f88-ab0426bce3ee.text: For a description of savings related to this program, see the Costs and Expenses––Restructuring Charges and Other Costs Associated with Acquisitions and Cost-Reduction/Productivity Initiatives section within MD&A .In July 2023, we announced that in consideration of planned future investments in oncology, including the proposed acquisition of Seagen, we are reorganizing how our R&D operations are conducted.

9c1e8b2b-21a4-463a-af2c-288c3585e520.label: text_chunk
9c1e8b2b-21a4-463a-af2c-288c3585e520.text: Beginning in July 2023, discovery to early- and late-phase clinical development for oncology will be performed by a new end-to-end Oncology Research and Development platform function and discovery to early- and late-phase clinical development for all remaining therapeutic areas will be consolidated in the Pfizer Research and Development platform function.For additional information about our business, strategy and operating environment, see the Item 1.

89ea6e65-6dc1-4421-bc58-e7185db0c2bb.label: text_chunk
89ea6e65-6dc1-4421-bc58-e7185db0c2bb.text: Business section and Overview of Our Performance, Operating Environment, Strategy and Outlook section within MD&A of our 2022 Form 10-K.Our Business Development Initiatives ––We are committed to strategically capitalizing on growth opportunities, primarily by advancing our own product pipeline and maximizing the value of our existing products, but also through various business development activities.

31002f2f-b3c8-4454-aa14-086a0919232c.label: text_chunk
31002f2f-b3c8-4454-aa14-086a0919232c.text:
Our significant recent business development activities include the transactions discussed in Notes 1A and 2 , including the proposed acquisition of Seagen, as well as the following:
Proposed Divestiture of Early-Stage Rare Disease Gene Therapy Portfolio – –In July 2023, we entered into an agreement with Alexion, a subsidiary of AstraZeneca plc, under which Alexion will purchase and license the assets of our early-stage rare disease gene therapy portfolio.

63175c79-12ad-49fa-ac5f-5f99a4b82aa5.label: text_chunk
63175c79-12ad-49fa-ac5f-5f99a4b82aa5.text: This agreement is consistent with our previously announced strategy to pivot from viral capsid-based gene therapy approaches to harnessing new platform technologies that we believe can have a transformative impact on patients, such as mRNA or in vivo gene editing. Under the terms of the agreement, Alexion will pay us total consideration of up to $1 billion, plus tiered royalties based on annual net sales of the assets.

b3bbbdf5-58a0-45f5-9c7b-65cbfe13df74.label: text_chunk
b3bbbdf5-58a0-45f5-9c7b-65cbfe13df74.text:
The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter of 2023, subject to customary closing conditions.

Agreement with Flagship Pioneering, Inc. (Flagship) – –In July 2023, we and Flagship announced that we have partnered to create a new pipeline of innovative medicines.

96c41c6c-b5fb-4b82-a1fc-46536f340fc3.label: text_chunk
96c41c6c-b5fb-4b82-a1fc-46536f340fc3.text:
Under the terms of the novel agreement, we and Flagship will each invest $50 million upfront to explore opportunities to develop 10 single-asset programs by leveraging Flagship’s ecosystem of more than 40 human health companies and multiple biotechnology platforms.

 Pfizer will fund and have an option to acquire each selected development program. Flagship and its bioplatform companies will be eligible to receive up to $700 million in milestones and royalties for each successfully commercialized program.38

0733c185-de19-49f9-96d2-bd5a01b840b1.label: text_chunk
0733c185-de19-49f9-96d2-bd5a01b840b1.text: Termination of Collaboration Arrangement with Merck KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany (Merck KGaA) – –In March 2023, it was announced that our alliance with Merck KGaA to co-develop and co-commercialize Bavencio (avelumab) will terminate. Effective June 30, 2023, Merck KGaA took full control of the global commercialization of Bavencio.

e0586a6e-fabf-4ce3-a8ba-2888639f1c18.label: text_chunk
e0586a6e-fabf-4ce3-a8ba-2888639f1c18.text: Beginning in the third quarter of 2023, the current profit share will be replaced by a 15% royalty to Pfizer on net sales of Bavencio. We and Merck KGaA will continue to operationalize our respective ongoing clinical trials for Bavencio; and Merck KGaA will control all future R&D activities.

33ebd471-b66e-4953-be3f-3e39a344c2cd.label: text_chunk
33ebd471-b66e-4953-be3f-3e39a344c2cd.text: For a description of the more significant recent transactions through February 23, 2023, the filing date of our 2022 Form 10-K, see Note 2 in our 2022 Form 10-K.

Our Second Quarter 2023 and First Six Months of 2023 Performance

Revenues –– Revenues decreased $15.0 billion, or 54%, in the second quarter of 2023 to $12.7 billion from $27.7 billion in the second quarter of 2022, reflecting an operational decrease of $14.7 billion, or 53%, as well as an unfavorable impact of foreign exchange of $283 million, or 1%.

d9ee6964-2b07-4525-9003-1a494584de2e.label: text_chunk
d9ee6964-2b07-4525-9003-1a494584de2e.text:
The operational decrease was primarily driven by declines in Paxlovid and Comirnaty.

 Excluding contributions from Comirnaty and Paxlovid, revenues increased $537 million, or 5%, operationally, reflecting revenues from recently acquired products, Nurtec ODT/Vydura and Oxbryta, and strong growth from the Vyndaqel family, partially offset by declines in Inflectra in the U.S. and Ibrance.Revenues decreased $22.

0bfd6ec7-4e3a-4400-be15-fd459df7ee7a.label: text_chunk
0bfd6ec7-4e3a-4400-be15-fd459df7ee7a.text: 4 billion, or 42%, in the first six months of 2023 to $31.0 billion from $53.4 billion in the first six months of 2022, reflecting an operational decrease of $21.4 billion, or 40%, as well as an unfavorable impact of foreign exchange of $1.0 billion, or 2%. The operational decrease was primarily driven by declines in Comirnaty and Paxlovid.

b775dab8-b9c5-4fb9-b00a-8a7f359e037b.label: text_chunk
b775dab8-b9c5-4fb9-b00a-8a7f359e037b.text: Excluding contributions from Comirnaty and Paxlovid, revenues increased $1.1 billion, or 5%, operationally, reflecting revenues from recently acquired products, Nurtec ODT/Vydura and Oxbryta, as well as strong growth from the Vyndaqel family and Eliquis, and increased Sulperazon revenues in China in the first quarter of 2023, partially offset by a decline in Ibrance.

c0278cb8-118a-4d73-b2d4-9714acbdb0b5.label: text_chunk
c0278cb8-118a-4d73-b2d4-9714acbdb0b5.text: As of August 1, 2023, on a total company basis, we forecasted revenues in 2023 of $67 billion to $70 billion, reflecting an operational decline of 31% at the midpoint from 2022 results, which we expect will also have an unfavorable impact on Income from continuing operations before provision/(benefit) for taxes on income .

f7be1ca4-f86b-4723-9c32-af1a13ba5ed5.label: text_chunk
f7be1ca4-f86b-4723-9c32-af1a13ba5ed5.text: Our Operating Environment ––We, like other businesses in our industry, are subject to certain industry-specific challenges. These include, among others, the topics listed below, as well as in the Item 1. Business––Government Regulation and Price Constraints and Item 1A.

51d4b582-fb77-4888-a867-1ac580f93889.label: text_chunk
51d4b582-fb77-4888-a867-1ac580f93889.text: Risk Factors sections, and the Overview of Our Performance, Operating Environment, Strategy and Outlook –– Our Operating Environment section of the MD&A of our 2022 Form 10-K and the Item 1A.

61d2c8fd-fa13-441b-98a8-34235a43c6cd.label: text_chunk
61d2c8fd-fa13-441b-98a8-34235a43c6cd.text: Risk Factors section of this Form 10-Q.Intellectual Property Rights and Collaboration/Licensing Rights ––The loss, expiration or invalidation of intellectual property rights, patent litigation settlements and the expiration of co-promotion and licensing rights can have a material adverse effect on our revenues. Certain of our products have experienced patent-based expirations or loss of regulatory exclusivity in certain markets in the last few years, and we expect certain products to face increased generic competition over the next few years.

d6660fe3-4684-48ea-a77b-98108cfc2a02.label: text_chunk
d6660fe3-4684-48ea-a77b-98108cfc2a02.text:
While additional patent expiries will continue, we expect a moderate impact of reduced revenues due to patent expiries from 2023 through 2025. We anticipate a more significant impact of reduced revenues from patent expiries in 2026 through 2030 as several of our in- line products experience patent-based expirations. We continue to vigorously defend our patent rights against
39

b61468a1-86a9-491b-8548-8b93e5cd3de9.label: text_chunk
b61468a1-86a9-491b-8548-8b93e5cd3de9.text: infringement, and we will continue to support efforts that strengthen worldwide recognition of patent rights while taking necessary steps to help ensure appropriate patient access.For additional information, see the Item 1. Business––Patents and Other Intellectual Property Rights and the Item 1A. Risk Factors––Intellectual Property Protection sections of our 2022 Form 10-K. For a discussion of recent developments with respect to patent litigation, see Note 12A1 .

229bd26a-1401-45cf-a2b0-20f90b579db5.label: text_chunk
229bd26a-1401-45cf-a2b0-20f90b579db5.text: Regulatory Environment/Pricing and Access––Government and Other Payer Group Pressures ––Governments globally, as well as private third-party payers in the U.S., may use a variety of measures to control costs, including, among others, proposing pricing reform or legislation, employing formularies to control costs, cross country collaboration and procurement, price cuts, mandatory rebates, health technology assessments, forced localization as a condition of market access, “international reference pricing” (i.

410d45d6-88d5-46f3-b4b3-e4236af4e1b2.label: text_chunk
410d45d6-88d5-46f3-b4b3-e4236af4e1b2.text: e., the practice of a country linking its regulated medicine prices to those of other countries), quality consistency evaluation processes and volume-based procurement. We anticipate that these and similar initiatives will continue to increase pricing and access pressures globally. In the U.S., we expect to see continued focus by Congress and the Biden Administration on regulating pricing, which could result in legislative and regulatory changes designed to control costs.

6222cbfa-f5e0-4b25-907b-4e8ee3395971.label: text_chunk
6222cbfa-f5e0-4b25-907b-4e8ee3395971.text: We continue to evaluate the impact of the IRA, which was signed into law in August 2022, on our business, operations and financial condition and results as the full effect of the IRA on our business and the pharmaceutical industry remains uncertain. In addition, changes to the Medicaid program or the federal 340B drug pricing program, including legal or legislative developments at the federal or state level with respect to the 340B program, could have a material impact on our business.

d5452a26-c981-4fac-8bdf-838fb33c5d1e.label: text_chunk
d5452a26-c981-4fac-8bdf-838fb33c5d1e.text: See the Item 1. Business –– Pricing Pressures and Managed Care Organizations and –– Government Regulation and Price Constraints and the Item 1A. Risk Factors––Pricing and Reimbursement sections, and the Overview of Our Performance, Operating Environment, Strategy and Outlook––Our Operating Environment section of the MD&A of our 2022 Form 10-K .

0126f414-e929-4bf7-944d-e702e2adac64.label: text_chunk
0126f414-e929-4bf7-944d-e702e2adac64.text: Impact of Recent Tornado in Rocky Mount, North Carolina (NC )–– Our manufacturing facility in Rocky Mount, NC was damaged by a tornado in July 2023. The facility is a key producer of sterile injectables and is responsible for manufacturing nearly 25 percent of all our sterile injectables—including anesthesia, analgesia, and micronutrients—which is nearly eight percent of all the sterile injectables used in U.S. hospitals.

f3ca7f05-558f-47a0-992a-c1312a380eaa.label: text_chunk
f3ca7f05-558f-47a0-992a-c1312a380eaa.text: The HiRise warehouse sustained major damage and all raw materials, packaging supplies and finished medicines stored within were damaged. All necessary supplies have been reordered for expedited delivery and we are actively engaging with suppliers. We are working diligently to move product stored within other areas of the facility not impacted by the event to other nearby sites for storage and to secure temporary warehouse space in the vicinity, as an interim solution, until the warehouse on the Rocky Mount campus can be rebuilt.

3dc5b328-c2ac-44f5-b731-9125005c899d.label: text_chunk
3dc5b328-c2ac-44f5-b731-9125005c899d.text:
We are also exploring alternative manufacturing locations for production across our significant manufacturing presence in the U.S. and internationally and across our partner network. After an initial assessment, there does not appear to be major damage to the production areas. We are committed to rapidly restoring full function to the site. We are providing financial support to help local communities affected by the natural disaster.

 We are currently evaluating the financial impact of the tornado on our business, including costs to repair and rebuild the site,

88eb964b-de3b-4972-9720-91e1db08b7ba.label: text_chunk
88eb964b-de3b-4972-9720-91e1db08b7ba.text: inventory losses, potential losses from stock outages at the hospital/retail level, overhead costs related to the period in which the plant may be inoperable, as well as other one-time remediation and/or other incremental costs which may be necessary as we work to bring our plant back online, but we are unable to predict the impact or insurance recoveries with certainty at this time.

956935e9-1215-4707-b7d5-4f1b9a723a57.label: text_chunk
956935e9-1215-4707-b7d5-4f1b9a723a57.text: Product Supply ––We periodically encounter supply delays, disruptions and shortages, including due to voluntary product recalls and natural or man-made disasters. In response to requests from various regulatory authorities, manufacturers across the pharmaceutical industry, including Pfizer, are evaluating their product portfolios for the potential presence or formation of nitrosamines.

a21d11ea-16bf-4073-ab12-708a9bd76523.label: text_chunk
a21d11ea-16bf-4073-ab12-708a9bd76523.text: This has led to recalls, including our voluntary recall of Chantix in 2021 and additional voluntary recalls initiated for other products in 2022 due to the presence of nitrosamines above the FDA interim acceptable intake limit, and may lead to additional recalls or other market actions for Pfizer products.

5f19aaa1-b7e1-42ee-9172-9bd3f4db61f6.label: text_chunk
5f19aaa1-b7e1-42ee-9172-9bd3f4db61f6.text: Except for the recent tornado in Rocky Mount, NC discussed above, we have not seen a significant disruption of our supply chain in the first six months of 2023 and to date, and all of our manufacturing sites globally have continued to operate at or near normal levels; however, we continue to see heightened demand in the industry for certain components and raw materials, which could potentially result in constraining available supply leading to a possible future impact on our business.

059a6deb-6169-4ed0-908d-3832316dd128.label: text_chunk
059a6deb-6169-4ed0-908d-3832316dd128.text: We are continuing to monitor and implement mitigation strategies in an effort to reduce any potential risk or impact including active supplier management, qualification of additional suppliers and advanced purchasing to the extent possible. For information on risks related to product manufacturing, see the Item 1A. Risk Factors––Product Manufacturing, Sales and Marketing Risks section of our 2022 Form 10-K.

e00cc4c7-521f-4ccd-be42-ebe3efee5884.label: text_chunk
e00cc4c7-521f-4ccd-be42-ebe3efee5884.text: The Global Economic Environment ––In addition to the industry-specific factors discussed above, we, like other businesses of our size and global extent of activities, are exposed to economic cycles. See the Overview of Our Performance, Operating Environment, Strategy and Outlook –– The Global Economic Environment section of the MD&A of our 2022 Form 10-K.

c92d6aaf-c110-4170-8c79-2fb7021b10a7.label: text_chunk
c92d6aaf-c110-4170-8c79-2fb7021b10a7.text:
COVID-19 ––In response to COVID-19, we have developed Paxlovid and collaborated with BioNTech to jointly develop Comirnaty, including, subject to approval, an Omicron XBB.1.5-adapted monovalent vaccine. As part of our strategy for
40
COVID-19, we are continuing to make significant additional investments in breakthrough science and global manufacturing.

f851c397-0249-4da5-8c9d-f3d8fb9ff34f.label: text_chunk
f851c397-0249-4da5-8c9d-f3d8fb9ff34f.text: This includes continuing to evaluate Comirnaty and Paxlovid, including against new variants of concern, developing variant adapted vaccine candidates and developing potential combination respiratory vaccines and potential next generation vaccines and therapies. We are also evaluating Paxlovid for additional populations. See the Product Developments section within MD&A.In the first six months of 2023 and to date, we principally sold Comirnaty globally under government contracts and Paxlovid globally to government agencies and distributors.

b9700225-8eb2-4cab-b1a9-bf7fcb5741e1.label: text_chunk
b9700225-8eb2-4cab-b1a9-bf7fcb5741e1.text: We expect Comirnaty in the U.S. will transition to traditional commercial market sales in the second half of 2023, triggered by the expiration of current contracts and the COVID-19 vaccines from Pfizer and BioNTech purchased through them becoming either depleted or not used following the introduction of a new variant vaccine.

cd82d722-ab41-474b-9ba2-c19f77515c61.label: text_chunk
cd82d722-ab41-474b-9ba2-c19f77515c61.text: Internationally, we expect sales of Comirnaty in international developed markets to generally be under government contracts in 2023, and in emerging markets, under a combination of private channels and government contracts; in both cases, we expect to generally transition to commercial markets starting in 2024. For Paxlovid, we expect to transition to traditional commercial markets in the second half of 2023 rather than significant government purchases. We anticipate this transition in the U.S.

ccd969a6-9008-4660-a8b4-9a87d3064372.label: text_chunk
ccd969a6-9008-4660-a8b4-9a87d3064372.text: to occur in the second half of 2023, though at this point we have not yet agreed on a transition plan with the U.S. government. We also remain committed to helping ensure broad and equitable access to our COVID-19 products to eligible patients around the world. Revenues from our COVID-19 products are expected to go from their peak in 2022 to their low point in 2023 before potentially returning to growth in 2024.

e1d28ee3-2c87-448d-b4a8-6e3453541858.label: text_chunk
e1d28ee3-2c87-448d-b4a8-6e3453541858.text: While patient demand for our COVID-19 products is expected to remain strong throughout 2023, much of that demand is expected to be fulfilled by existing supply of products that were delivered to governments and recorded as revenues in 2022. As of August 1, 2023, we forecasted Comirnaty revenues of approximately $13.

c529b519-bea1-4dfb-8a48-e694fee67312.label: text_chunk
c529b519-bea1-4dfb-8a48-e694fee67312.text:
5 billion in 2023, down 64% from actual 2022 results, with gross profit to be split evenly with BioNTech, and Paxlovid revenues of approximately $8 billion in 2023, down 58% from actual 2022 results.

 These forecasts are based on estimates and assumptions that are subject to significant uncertainties, including, among others, patient demand,

ec4c7c17-d905-4221-a8b9-f38d3194cdb2.label: text_chunk
ec4c7c17-d905-4221-a8b9-f38d3194cdb2.text:
which could be significantly impacted by the infectiousness and severity of the predominant strains of the SARS-CoV-2 virus during 2023, proportion of the population that receives a vaccine or is treated with an oral antiviral treatment, the number of doses per vaccinated person per year, number of symptomatic infections, market share of Comirnaty and Paxlovid and the timing for transitioning Comirnaty and Paxlovid to commercial markets in the U.

S.

73d7904d-6cf8-4bfb-a2c6-903a03dcc24f.label: text_chunk
73d7904d-6cf8-4bfb-a2c6-903a03dcc24f.text: For information on the impact of COVID-19 on our business, operations and financial conditions and results and risks associated with COVID-19 and our COVID-19 products, as well as COVID-19 intellectual property disputes, see the Item 1A.

c4c89852-4496-4d02-9d30-cd0b6e373736.label: text_chunk
c4c89852-4496-4d02-9d30-cd0b6e373736.text: Russia/Ukraine Conflict ––Our global operations may be impacted by the armed conflict between Russia and Ukraine.

b7f63fa7-ec50-4616-801f-66e7b0d0285f.label: text_chunk
b7f63fa7-ec50-4616-801f-66e7b0d0285f.text:
For both the six months ended July 2, 2023 and the fiscal year ended December 31, 2022, the business of our Russia and Ukraine subsidiaries represented less than 1% of our consolidated revenues and assets, and while we are monitoring the effects of the armed conflict between Russia and Ukraine, the situation continues to evolve and the long-term implications, including the broader economic consequences of the conflict, are difficult to predict at this time.

 While as of now,

eb6c0aec-d517-4857-9455-28f32dd42833.label: text_chunk
eb6c0aec-d517-4857-9455-28f32dd42833.text: we do not anticipate any significant negative impacts on our business from this conflict, continued regional instability, geopolitical shifts, potential additional sanctions and other restrictive measures against Russia, neighboring countries or allies of Russia, any retaliatory measures taken by Russia, neighboring countries or allies of Russia, and actions by our customers or suppliers, including financial institutions, in response to such measures could adversely affect the global macroeconomic environment, our operations, currency exchange rates and financial markets,

24942af5-2f13-400e-9fbe-fcb05da6bfe8.label: text_chunk
24942af5-2f13-400e-9fbe-fcb05da6bfe8.text:
which could in turn adversely impact our business and results of operations.

 For additional information on our response to the armed conflict between Russia and Ukraine as well as risks associated with the conflict, see the Item 1A. Risk Factors—Global Operations section and the Overview of Our Performance, Operating Environment, Strategy and Outlook section of the MD&A of our 2022 Form 10-K.

Transforming to a More Focused Company Program ––For a description of our program, as well as the anticipated and actual costs, see Note 3A . A comprehensive update of Pfizer’s development pipeline was published as of August 1, 2023 and is available at www.pfizer.com/science/drug-product- pipeline. For additional information about our R&D organization, see the Overview of Our Performance, Operating Environment, Strategy and Outlook –– Our Business and Strategy section within MD&A and Item 1. Business — Research and Development section of our 2022 Form 10-K.

NON-GAAP FINANCIAL MEASURE: ADJUSTED INCOME

Adjusted income is an alternative measure of performance used by management to evaluate our overall performance as a supplement to our GAAP Reported performance measures.

34ce4ffd-ecd6-4f55-89c2-b3b870f69217.label: text_chunk
34ce4ffd-ecd6-4f55-89c2-b3b870f69217.text: As such, we believe that investors’ understanding of our performance is enhanced by disclosing this measure.

bb07eda6-cb98-4405-a452-644dbd292fbf.label: text_chunk
bb07eda6-cb98-4405-a452-644dbd292fbf.text:
We use Adjusted income, certain components of Adjusted income and Adjusted diluted EPS to present the results of our major operations––the discovery, development, manufacture, marketing, sale and distribution of biopharmaceutical products worldwide––prior to considering certain income statement elements as follows:

8e8f011c-3c55-47d0-9504-4a06c8ceb88b.text:
Measure Definition Relevance of Metrics to Our Business Performance Adjusted income Net income attributable to Pfizer Inc. common shareholders before the impact of amortization of intangible assets, certain acquisition-related items, discontinued operations and certain significant items • Provides investors useful information to:

◦ evaluate the normal recurring operational activities, and their components,

on a comparable year- over-year basis

◦ assist in modeling expected future performance on a normalized basis

• Provides investors insight into the way we manage our budgeting and forecasting,

 how we evaluate and manage our recurring operations and how we reward and compensate our senior management
Adjusted cost of sales, Adjusted selling, informational and administrative expenses, Adjusted research and development expenses and Adjusted other (income)/deductions –– net

ff4abff2-80c6-42ba-82a1-e639707c0d57.text:
Cost of sales, Selling, informational and administrative expenses, Research and development expenses and Other (income)/deductions––net , each before the impact of amortization of intangible assets, certain acquisition-related items, discontinued operations and certain significant items, which are components of the Adjusted income measure
Adjusted diluted EPS EPS attributable to Pfizer Inc.

afbd7c33-5f1c-41b1-ba1e-f9a762f41150.label: text_chunk
afbd7c33-5f1c-41b1-ba1e-f9a762f41150.text:
common shareholders––diluted before the impact of amortization of intangible assets, certain acquisition-related items, discontinued operations and certain significant items

Most directly comparable GAAP measure.

8c9b1dc9-9795-4a0e-92e3-ff4cb5b55b53.label: text_chunk
8c9b1dc9-9795-4a0e-92e3-ff4cb5b55b53.text: The short-term incentive plans for substantially all non-sales-force employees worldwide are funded from a pool based on our performance, measured in significant part versus three budgeted metrics, one of which is Adjusted diluted EPS (as defined for annual incentive compensation purposes), which is derived from Adjusted income and accounts for 40% of the bonus pool funding tied to financial performance.

eaa9357d-f55e-4fb0-bd08-77216a782610.label: text_chunk
eaa9357d-f55e-4fb0-bd08-77216a782610.text:
Additionally, the payout for performance share awards is determined in part by Adjusted net income, which is derived from Adjusted income.

 Beginning in the first quarter of 2022, we no longer exclude any expenses for acquired IPR&D from our non-GAAP Adjusted results but we continue to exclude certain of these expenses for our financial results for annual incentive compensation purposes.

b0fdcb3c-5ffa-4426-a84f-e78e8ac0f0f1.label: text_chunk
b0fdcb3c-5ffa-4426-a84f-e78e8ac0f0f1.text: The bonus pool funding, which is largely based on financial performance, is adjusted by our R&D pipeline performance, as measured by four metrics, and performance against certain of our ESG metrics, and may be further modified by our Compensation Committee’s assessment of other factors.Adjusted income and its components and Adjusted diluted EPS are non-GAAP financial measures that have no standardized meaning prescribed by GAAP and, therefore, are limited in their usefulness to investors.

d5a2d188-d196-4d26-af04-e8287a5dc860.label: text_chunk
d5a2d188-d196-4d26-af04-e8287a5dc860.text: Because of their non-standardized definitions, they may not be comparable to the calculation of similar measures of other companies and are presented to permit investors to more fully understand how management assesses performance. A limitation of these measures is that they provide a view of our operations without including all events during a period, and do not provide a comparable view of our performance to peers.

ffec46bf-d256-49ac-9bef-54c92f203aa0.label: text_chunk
ffec46bf-d256-49ac-9bef-54c92f203aa0.text: These measures are not, and should not be viewed as, substitutes for their most directly comparable GAAP measures of Net income attributable to Pfizer Inc. common shareholders , components of Net income attributable to Pfizer Inc. common shareholders and EPS attributable to Pfizer Inc. common shareholders—diluted , respectively.We also recognize that, as internal measures of performance, these measures have limitations, and we do not restrict our performance-management process solely to these measures.

e4a3a3a2-2bd7-4d8c-b473-18d5e305edb9.label: text_chunk
e4a3a3a2-2bd7-4d8c-b473-18d5e305edb9.text: We also use other tools designed to achieve the highest levels of performance. For example, our R&D organization has productivity targets, upon which its effectiveness is measured. In addition, total shareholder return, both on an absolute basis and relative to a publicly traded pharmaceutical index, plays a significant role in determining payouts under certain of our incentive compensation plans.

b18771f5-d338-4ffd-af90-76fdaa9acf25.label: text_chunk
b18771f5-d338-4ffd-af90-76fdaa9acf25.text:
Adjusted Income and Adjusted Diluted EPS

Amortization of Intangible Assets —Adjusted income excludes all amortization of intangible assets.Acquisition-Related Items —Adjusted income excludes certain acquisition-related items, which are comprised of transaction, integration, restructuring charges and additional depreciation costs for business combinations because these costs are unique to each transaction and represent costs that were incurred to restructure and integrate businesses as a result of an acquisition. We have made no adjustments for resulting synergies.

116fe528-5658-4ad5-b19a-e2edb98c3510.label: text_chunk
116fe528-5658-4ad5-b19a-e2edb98c3510.text: Acquisition-related items may include purchase accounting impacts such as the incremental charge to cost of sales from the sale of acquired inventory that was written up to fair value, depreciation related to the increase/decrease in fair value of acquired fixed assets, amortization related to the increase in fair value of acquired debt, and the fair value changes for contingent consideration.Discontinued Operations —Adjusted income excludes the results of discontinued operations, as well as any related gains or losses on the disposal of such operations.

e8cf3343-4753-4706-ad6a-0e8a8685eebd.label: text_chunk
e8cf3343-4753-4706-ad6a-0e8a8685eebd.text:
We believe that this presentation is meaningful to investors because, while we review our product portfolio for strategic fit with our operations, we do not build or run our business with the intent to discontinue parts of our business. Restatements due to discontinued operations do not impact compensation or change the Adjusted income measure for the compensation in respect of the restated periods, but are presented for consistency across all periods.(a)

baf7e90e-f4ab-4e40-901b-7278228841a0.label: text_chunk
baf7e90e-f4ab-4e40-901b-7278228841a0.text: Certain Significant Items —Adjusted income excludes certain significant items representing substantive and/or unusual items that are evaluated individually on a quantitative and qualitative basis. Certain significant items may be highly variable and difficult to predict. Furthermore, in some cases it is reasonably possible that they could reoccur in future periods.

105807b4-4b09-4b2d-a57d-f22fb9809666.label: text_chunk
105807b4-4b09-4b2d-a57d-f22fb9809666.text: For example, although major non-acquisition-related cost-reduction programs are specific to an event or goal with a defined term, we may have subsequent programs based on reorganizations of the business, cost productivity or in response to LOE or economic conditions.

a49aea59-2aef-4eda-8438-d93afdbe2637.text: Legal charges to resolve litigation are also related to specific cases, which are facts and circumstances specific and, in some cases, may also be the result of litigation matters at acquired companies that were inestimable, not probable or unresolved at the date of acquisition, or legal matters related to divested products or businesses.

4b06509c-dff9-48ab-a56b-330bd950da89.label: text_chunk
4b06509c-dff9-48ab-a56b-330bd950da89.text: Gains and losses on equity securities, and pension and postretirement actuarial remeasurement gains and losses have a very high degree of inherent market volatility, which we do not control and cannot predict with any level of certainty and because we do not believe including these gains and losses assists investors in understanding our business or is reflective of our core operations and business.

f9490438-c8d9-413f-95cb-ccaca9b8b303.label: text_chunk
f9490438-c8d9-413f-95cb-ccaca9b8b303.text: Unusual items represent items that are not part of our ongoing business; items that, either as a result of their nature or size, we would not expect to occur as part of our normal business on a regular basis; items that would be non-recurring; or items that relate to products we no longer sell.

b3c42602-e092-4d4a-beba-2571a6bdddd2.text: We are including the following risk factors, which should be read in conjunction with the risk factors discussed in the Item 1A. Risk Factors section of our 2022 Form 10-K.

0c02efb5-e938-42ce-9ac9-1b53df3fc6f5.label: text_chunk
0c02efb5-e938-42ce-9ac9-1b53df3fc6f5.text:
PROPOSED ACQUISITION OF SEAGEN

We may be unable to complete the acquisition of Seagen within the anticipated timeframe or at all, which could prevent us from receiving the anticipated benefits from the acquisition in the anticipated timeframe or at all.On March 12, 2023, we entered into a merger agreement with Seagen.

a4f0ed80-9ce8-4c20-80ba-1db55b46fef4.label: text_chunk
a4f0ed80-9ce8-4c20-80ba-1db55b46fef4.text:
The transaction is expected to close in late 2023 or early 2024, and remains subject to customary closing conditions, including receipt of required regulatory approvals.

 As a result, there is no assurance that the acquisition will be consummated in the anticipated timeframe or at all.

afc05f51-17b8-40bc-9284-b911fff3ec76.label: text_chunk
afc05f51-17b8-40bc-9284-b911fff3ec76.text:
In addition, Pfizer may be required to pay Seagen a reverse termination fee of approximately $2.22 billion, subject to certain limitations set forth in the merger agreement, if the merger agreement is terminated by either party as a result of certain antitrust and/or foreign direct investment law-related conditions.

 Any failure to consummate the acquisition in the anticipated timeframe or at all could prevent Pfizer from receiving the expected benefits from the acquisition.

0f232324-5654-4a6e-8625-951af7b31772.label: text_chunk
0f232324-5654-4a6e-8625-951af7b31772.text: See the Overview of Our Performance, Operating Environment, Strategy and Outlook—Our Business Development Initiatives and the Forward-Looking Information and Factors That May Affect Future Results sections within MD&A.We have expended and will continue to expend significant time and resources in connection with the acquisition of Seagen and have incurred substantial indebtedness to fund the acquisition.

6dc494c3-47aa-4f72-9e45-82fb22c03767.label: text_chunk
6dc494c3-47aa-4f72-9e45-82fb22c03767.text: Pfizer has expended and will continue to expend significant management time and resources and expenses related to the acquisition of Seagen, many of which must be paid regardless of whether the acquisition is consummated. For example, such time, resources and expenses will be incurred in connection with seeking regulatory approvals for the transaction.

6294e9f6-4433-4361-8a0f-22fa5b06884a.label: text_chunk
6294e9f6-4433-4361-8a0f-22fa5b06884a.text: We intend to finance a portion of the transaction with the proceeds from the $31 billion of long-term debt issued in May 2023, plus additional short-term indebtedness to be issued prior to the acquisition, which indebtedness may limit our operating or financial flexibility relative to our current position.

46338b1e-1948-4fe9-a9ad-f03cefbaf1c0.label: text_chunk
46338b1e-1948-4fe9-a9ad-f03cefbaf1c0.text: We may not be successful in identifying and executing potential business development transactions, such as our acquisition of Seagen, or realizing the financial and strategic goals that were contemplated at the time of any historical or potential business development transaction, which could have an adverse impact on our ability to meet our growth objectives.

8640c11e-5fcc-4ff7-b3cb-57967f8c8b51.label: text_chunk
8640c11e-5fcc-4ff7-b3cb-57967f8c8b51.text: We have established significant growth goals, which we plan to achieve, in part, by accelerating revenue growth by not only advancing our own product pipelines and maximizing the value of our existing products, but also through various forms of business development activities, which can include alliances, licenses, JVs, collaborations, equity- or debt-based investments, dispositions, divestments, mergers and acquisitions. Our proposed acquisition of Seagen is part of that accelerated revenue growth plan.

6afed00c-cd47-4f93-8096-d8281e134939.label: text_chunk
6afed00c-cd47-4f93-8096-d8281e134939.text: We view our business development activity as an enabler of our strategies and seek to generate growth by pursuing opportunities and transactions that have the potential to strengthen our business and our capabilities.

5993daea-2b03-4603-b522-58de9e767b97.label: text_chunk
5993daea-2b03-4603-b522-58de9e767b97.text: The success of our business development activities is dependent on the availability and accurate evaluation of appropriate opportunities, competition from others that are seeking similar opportunities and our ability to successfully identify, structure and execute transactions, including the ability to satisfy or waive closing conditions in the anticipated timeframes, or at all, and our ability to successfully integrate acquired businesses and develop and commercialize acquired products.

0d934837-d090-47a1-b9e0-08817316a264.label: text_chunk
0d934837-d090-47a1-b9e0-08817316a264.text:
Pursuing, executing and consummating these transactions may require substantial investment, which may require us to obtain additional equity or debt financing, which could result in increased leverage and/or a downgrade of our credit ratings or limit our operating or financial flexibility relative to our current position. The success of our business development transactions depends on our ability to realize the anticipated benefits of these transactions and is subject to numerous risks and uncertainties, many of which are
60

ab142932-102a-40ba-bb0c-28421e7bcd83.label: text_chunk
ab142932-102a-40ba-bb0c-28421e7bcd83.text: outside of our control, including the possibility that the expected benefits from such transactions will not be realized or will not be realized within the expected time period. Unsuccessful clinical trials, regulatory hurdles and commercialization challenges may adversely impact revenue and income contribution from acquired products and businesses.

34c6344e-0793-4718-a2a7-13a90cbe16e5.label: text_chunk
34c6344e-0793-4718-a2a7-13a90cbe16e5.text: We may fail to generate expected revenue growth for our existing products, product pipeline and acquired products or businesses or we may fail to achieve anticipated cost savings, such as those expected with respect to Seagen, within expected time frames or at all, which may impact our ability to meet our growth objectives. In certain transactions, we may agree to provide certain transition services for an extended period of time, which may divert our focus and resources that would otherwise be invested into maintaining or growing our business.

1461a9b3-b934-403e-908a-f7c15a540ef2.label: text_chunk
1461a9b3-b934-403e-908a-f7c15a540ef2.text: Similarly, the accretive impact anticipated from transactions may not be realized or may be delayed. Integration of these products or businesses may result in the loss of key employees, the disruption of ongoing business, including third-party relationships, or inconsistencies in standards, controls, procedures and policies. Further, while we seek to mitigate risks and liabilities through, among other things, due diligence, we may be exposed to risks and liabilities as a result of business development transactions.

50774863-06b4-4af5-8b15-bd4e3bca1aed.label: text_chunk
50774863-06b4-4af5-8b15-bd4e3bca1aed.text:
There is no assurance that we will be able to acquire attractive businesses or enter into strategic business relationships on favorable terms ahead of our competitors, or that such acquisitions or strategic business development relationships will be accretive to earnings or improve our competitive position.

EXHIBIT 10.1

PFIZER INC.

EXECUTIVE OFFICER CASH SEVERANCE POLICY

Pfizer Inc.

a25feba6-7c15-4c37-b833-15ae0114a46e.label: text_chunk
a25feba6-7c15-4c37-b833-15ae0114a46e.text: (the “Company”) will not enter into any new employment agreement, severance agreement or separation agreement with any executive officer of the Company or establish any new severance plan or policy covering any executive officer of the Company, in each case that provides for Cash Severance Benefits exceeding 2.99 times the sum of the executive officer’s Base Salary and Target Bonus, without seeking stockholder ratification of such agreement, plan or policy.For purposes of this policy:

18332d1d-f6c7-4d17-8a0d-2e2eb23a8464.label: text_chunk
18332d1d-f6c7-4d17-8a0d-2e2eb23a8464.text: “Cash Severance Benefits” means cash payments: (i) in respect of the termination of the executive officer’s employment; (ii) to secure an agreement not to compete with the Company; or (iii) to offset any tax liability in respect of any of the foregoing.

3d879b78-ff6c-4ddb-b0b8-9089ec4ec1fb.label: text_chunk
3d879b78-ff6c-4ddb-b0b8-9089ec4ec1fb.text:
For the avoidance of doubt, “Cash Severance Benefits” do not include (a) the grant, payment, vesting, acceleration or other handling of long term incentive awards granted under the Company’s stockholder-approved plans, including without limitation the Pfizer Inc. 2014 Stock Plan, the Pfizer Inc.

 2019 Stock Plan or any successor plans, (b) payment of deferred compensation, earned retirement benefits or other vested employee benefits, in each case consistent with normal practices,

872d0d45-3877-4dc8-b5a4-be4b42f42725.label: text_chunk
872d0d45-3877-4dc8-b5a4-be4b42f42725.text: provided under the Company’s qualified or nonqualified retirement plans or employee benefit plans, (c) the provision of perquisites, insurance, disability, health and welfare plan coverage, outplacement or retraining and other non-cash benefits generally available to similarly-situated employees, (d) any notice pay or interest required to be paid pursuant to the terms of any Company plan or policy between the termination date and the payment date,

bdef6320-c9ed-4ab5-94df-276308b63a7f.label: text_chunk
bdef6320-c9ed-4ab5-94df-276308b63a7f.text: (e) any unpaid bonus for any previously completed performance period required to be paid pursuant to the terms of any Company plan or policy, (f) accrued but unpaid base salary or vacation pay through the termination date and reimbursement for any expenses validly incurred prior to the termination date or (g) any payment in respect of the executive officer’s bonus for the year of termination (prorated based on the executive officer’s completed service during the annual performance period).

7ef55c18-03de-4a4f-a21f-d07f267117d9.label: text_chunk
7ef55c18-03de-4a4f-a21f-d07f267117d9.text: “Base Salary” means the annualized base salary in effect immediately prior to an executive officer’s termination date, (or, if higher, the highest annualized base salary in effect during the 12-month period prior to the termination date).“Target Bonus” means the executive officer’s target annual incentive bonus in effect for the year of termination.Exclusions apply for pre-existing agreements and arrangements assumed by the Company in the context of a corporate transaction .

August 9, 2023
This certification accompanies this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q pursuant to Section 906 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 and shall not, except to the extent required by such Act, be deemed filed by the Company for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “Exchange Act”).

community_summary.300:
**Summary of Relationships Involving Pfizer:**

1. **Regulatory Obligations**: Pfizer files Form 10-Q to report its quarterly financial performance to the SEC, fulfilling its regulatory requirements.

2. **Financial Performance and Liabilities**: In 2022, Pfizer incurred product liability expenses, reflecting its financial obligations during that period. Additionally, it faces ongoing tax audits by the IRS for the years 2016-2018.

3. **Collaborative Agreements**: Pfizer engages with financial institutions to facilitate supplier finance programs and has facilitated agreements between suppliers and these institutions. It also has a collaboration with Biohaven for commercialization rights outside the U.S. and partnered with BioNTech to develop the COVID-19 vaccine, Comirnaty.

4. **Product Development and Manufacturing**: Pfizer developed Lipitor, allowing Ranbaxy to produce a generic version. It historically manufactured Zantac and currently produces Chantix, both of which are subjects of litigation regarding health risks. Pfizer is also involved in the development of the COVID-19 vaccine and has submitted regulatory applications to the FDA and EMA for vaccine approvals.

5. **Legal Matters**: Pfizer is involved in various legal issues, including pending lawsuits with American Optical related to personal injury claims and allegations of conspiracy with Mylan to delay generic EpiPen market entry. It has also agreed to indemnify Viatris for liabilities.

6. **Financial Relationships**: Pfizer has a royalty agreement with Bavencio, entitling it to a 15% royalty on net sales. It has financial liabilities related to legal expenses concerning products like Haleon, which was spun off from GSK.

7. **Safety and Compliance Issues**: Pfizer initiated a voluntary recall of Chantix due to safety concerns and is alleged to have violated the U.S. Anti-Terrorism Act through its sales practices.

8. **Credit Ratings**: Pfizer's creditworthiness is evaluated by S&P, which assigned it a short-term rating of A-1 and a long-term rating of A+.

This summary encapsulates Pfizer's multifaceted relationships, highlighting its regulatory compliance, financial engagements, collaborative efforts in product development, legal challenges, and credit evaluations.

community_summary.326:
The relationships outlined highlight various financial concepts and instruments that are integral to understanding a company's financial health and performance. Here’s a summary of the key entities and their relationships:

1. **Condensed Consolidated Statements of Income & Condensed Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive Income**: Both are essential financial statements that provide insights into a company's financial performance, with one focusing specifically on income and the other encompassing comprehensive income.

2. **Net Periodic Benefit Costs & Depreciation**: These terms represent financial costs recorded in a company's financial statements, reflecting the company's obligations and strategies for asset management.

3. **Other Current Liabilities & Other Noncurrent Liabilities**: These categories of liabilities on a balance sheet indicate different time frames for settlement, distinguishing between short-term and long-term obligations.

4. **Marketable Securities & Long-term Debt**: Marketable securities are considered invested assets that can influence long-term debt, which is a significant financial liability for the company.

5. **Held-to-Maturity Debt Securities & Time Deposits**: Both are financial instruments that involve fund management and investment strategies, often used for securing returns over time.

6. **Government and Agency Securities & Held-to-Maturity Debt Securities**: Government and agency securities can be classified as held-to-maturity when they are intended to be retained until maturity, indicating a long-term investment strategy.

7. **Gains/(Losses) Recognized in OID & Gains/(Losses) Recognized in OCI**: These metrics reflect the impact of hedging activities on financial performance, with one recognized in Other Income and Expenses (OID) and the other in Other Comprehensive Income (OCI).

8. **Gains/(Losses) Reclassified from OCI into OID and COS & Gains/(Losses) Recognized in OCI**: This relationship illustrates how gains or losses in OCI can be reclassified into OID and Cost of Sales (COS), demonstrating the interconnectedness of financial reporting over time.

This summary encapsulates the critical aspects of the relationships, emphasizing their significance in financial reporting and analysis.

The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight various financial components and their contributions to broader financial categories within a company. Here’s a summary of the key entities and their relationships:

1. **Long-term Debt** is a significant part of **Total Liabilities**, indicating that it contributes to the overall financial obligations of a company.

2. **Trade Accounts Payable** is included in **Total Current Liabilities**, representing a portion of the company's short-term financial obligations.

3. **Dividends Payable** also forms part of **Total Current Liabilities**, reflecting the company's commitment to pay declared dividends to its shareholders.

4. **Cash and Cash Equivalents** encompass **Restricted Cash**, which is included in total liquid assets but is not available for immediate use.

5. **Implementation Costs** are integral to **Total Implementation Costs**, contributing to the overall expenses incurred during the implementation phase.

The relationships outlined highlight various treatments and their specific medical indications, emphasizing the direct connections between drugs and the diseases they target:

1. **Bosulif** is indicated for the treatment of **Philadelphia chromosome-positive chronic myelogenous leukemia**, specifically targeting this type of leukemia.
   
2. **Lorbrena** is used for treating **ALK-positive metastatic non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC)**, establishing a direct therapeutic relationship with this cancer type.

3. **Immunotherapy** serves as a treatment option for **advanced Renal Cell Carcinoma (RCC)**, enhancing the body's immune response against this cancer.

4. **Aromasin** is specifically utilized in the treatment of **hormone receptor-positive breast cancer** in postmenopausal women, directly addressing this form of breast cancer.

5. **Erbitux** is employed in combination with other therapies for the treatment of **metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC)**, indicating its role in managing this condition.

6. **Sutent** is indicated for the treatment of **advanced renal cell carcinoma (RCC)**, reinforcing its direct relationship with this disease.

7. **Fidanacogene elaparvovec** is a gene therapy designed for treating **Hemophilia B**, targeting the genetic basis of the disorder.

This summary encapsulates the critical aspects of each treatment and its corresponding medical condition, illustrating the significance of these relationships in the context of targeted therapies.

### Summary of Relationships

1. **Other Comprehensive Income & Balance**: Other Comprehensive Income contributes to the total equity reported in an entity's financial statements, thereby affecting its overall financial position.

2. **Unrealized Holding Gains/Losses on Derivative Financial Instruments & Reclassification Adjustments**: These unrealized gains/losses can necessitate adjustments in financial reporting, impacting net income.

3. **Unrealized Holding Gains/Losses on Available-for-Sale Securities & Reclassification Adjustments**: Similar to derivatives, these gains/losses influence reclassification adjustments, affecting net income when securities are sold or reclassified.

4. **Fair Value Hedging Adjustment & Carrying Amount**: This adjustment directly alters the recognized value of hedged assets or liabilities on the balance sheet.

5. **Curtailments & Net Periodic Benefit Cost**: Curtailments reduce overall employee benefit expenses, directly impacting the net periodic benefit cost.

6. **Actuarial Remeasurement Losses & Net Periodic Benefit Cost**: These losses are a significant factor in determining the net periodic benefit cost, affecting overall financial performance related to employee benefits.

7. **Common Stock Equivalents & Diluted EPS**: Common stock equivalents are included in the calculation of diluted earnings per share (EPS), potentially diluting the reported earnings.

8. **Income & Taxes**: Taxes directly reduce the net income a company retains, establishing a significant relationship between income and tax obligations.

9. **IRA & 340B Drug Pricing Program**: Changes in legislation through the IRA can impact drug pricing and availability, which are central to the 340B program.

10. **Deductions & Gross Sales**: Deductions adjust total revenue calculations, reflecting obligations and estimates that affect gross sales figures.

11. **Cost-Reduction Programs & Business**: Businesses implement cost-reduction programs to manage expenses and enhance financial performance.

12. **Actuarial Valuation & Income Tax Provision**: Changes in the valuation of pension and postretirement plans can lead to adjustments in the income tax expense recognized by a company.

13. **Legislation & Pharmaceutical Product Pricing**: Legislative measures can dictate how pharmaceutical prices are set and adjusted, directly influencing product pricing in the market.

This summary encapsulates the critical relationships between various financial elements, highlighting their interconnectedness and the implications for financial reporting and performance.

8. **Portfolio Diversification**: This strategy is a crucial element of an **Investment Strategy**, aimed at managing risk and enhancing returns through a diversified investment approach.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the interconnectedness of various financial components within a company's accounting and financial reporting framework, emphasizing the importance of each element in understanding the company's financial health and obligations.

### Summary of Relationships

1. **ACA and ABO**: The Affordable Care Act (ACA) significantly influences healthcare legislation, which in turn affects financial terms like Average Benefit Obligation (ABO) related to healthcare benefits.

2. **Outsourcing and Joint Ventures**: Outsourcing is connected to joint ventures as both involve collaboration with third parties, potentially leading to operational challenges and complexities.

3. **VBP and LOE**: Value-Based Purchasing (VBP) is impacted by Loss of Exclusivity (LOE), as drugs that lose exclusivity may need to meet VBP criteria for qualification.

4. **Critical Audit Matters and Consolidated Financial Statements**: Critical audit matters are discussed in the context of consolidated financial statements, providing additional insights into significant issues identified during audits without changing the auditor's opinion.

5. **Liabilities and Tax Positions**: Liabilities are closely linked to tax positions, representing financial obligations that arise from uncertain tax situations a company may encounter.

6. **Reclassification Adjustments and Curtailments**: Reclassification adjustments are often required when there are curtailments in benefit plans, reflecting necessary changes in the financial reporting of benefits.

7. **7 Billion and Asset Impairments**: The amount of 7 billion is associated with asset impairments, indicating the total loss recognized in financial reporting due to these impairments.

8. **Consolidated Financial Statements and 2020**: The consolidated financial statements encompass financial data specifically for the year 2020.

9. **Scheduled Payments and Redemption Notes**: Scheduled payments are directly tied to redemption notes, representing future cash flows that are essential for calculating the redemption price of these financial instruments. 

This summary encapsulates the key entities and their interrelated financial and operational implications, highlighting the significance of each relationship within the broader context of financial reporting and healthcare legislation.

community_summary.574:
The summary of relationships highlights the interconnectedness of various regulatory bodies and their oversight roles in both the pharmaceutical and financial sectors:

1. **European Commission and European Medicines Agency**: The European Commission oversees the European Medicines Agency, which is tasked with evaluating and supervising medicinal products within the European Union.

2. **Earnings Per Share (EPS) and Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB)**: EPS, a key financial metric, is shaped by the accounting standards established by FASB, which provides guidelines for earnings reporting.

3. **Pfizer Global Supply and Pharmaceuticals and Medical Device Agency**: Pfizer Global Supply operates under the regulations of the Pharmaceuticals and Medical Device Agency in Japan, ensuring compliance in the manufacturing and distribution of pharmaceutical products.

4. **Drug-Pricing Initiatives and Affordability Review Boards**: Affordability review boards play a crucial role in overseeing drug-pricing initiatives, which aim to enhance access to medications.

5. **FFDCA and Medicaid Drug Rebate Program**: Both the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) and the Medicaid Drug Rebate Program are regulatory frameworks that govern the pharmaceutical industry, ensuring adherence to safety and pricing standards.

6. **Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (PCAOB)**: The SEC and PCAOB are regulatory entities that oversee different facets of financial markets; the SEC focuses on securities laws while the PCAOB is concerned with the auditing of public companies.

This summary encapsulates the essential roles and relationships among regulatory bodies, emphasizing their significance in maintaining standards and compliance in their respective fields.

The relationships outlined highlight key aspects of Japan's healthcare system and its economic interactions, particularly in relation to Canada and the Comirnaty vaccine.

1. **Healthcare Systems**: Both Japan and Canada are noted for providing healthcare services at low-to-zero direct costs to consumers, showcasing a significant similarity in their healthcare approaches.

2. **Market Position**: Japan has been identified as the largest national market outside the U.S. in terms of total revenues as of 2021, emphasizing its economic significance on a global scale.

3. **Revenue Contribution**: Japan has a notable financial relationship with the Comirnaty vaccine, contributing 9% of total revenue in 2021 and maintaining a contribution of 6% in the preceding years (2020 and 2019). This indicates Japan's important role in the vaccine's market performance.

Overall, the summary underscores Japan's prominent position in both healthcare provision and as a significant market contributor, particularly in the context of pharmaceutical revenues.

### Summary of Relationships

1. **Purchase Accounting Adjustments**:
   - Involves **Finite-Lived Intangible Assets**: These adjustments include amortization related to the fair value increase of acquired finite-lived intangible assets.

2. **Defined Benefit Pension Plans**:
   - Part of **Postretirement Benefit Plans**: Defined benefit pension plans are included within the broader category of postretirement benefits offered by organizations.

3. **Postretirement Benefit Plans**:
   - Include **Medical Insurance Benefits**: These plans often encompass medical insurance benefits as part of the overall retirement package.

4. **Total Assets and Total Liabilities**:
   - Total assets consist of both current and noncurrent assets, while total liabilities include current and noncurrent liabilities, providing a comprehensive view of a company's financial position.

5. **Program Costs**:
   - Include **Implementation Costs**: Implementation costs are recognized as part of the total expenses associated with executing a program.

6. **Total Implementation Costs**:
   - Likely include **Total Costs Associated with Acquisitions**: These costs are part of the overall expenses incurred during project implementation.

7. **Total Other Noncurrent Assets**:
   - Include **Insurance Contracts**: Insurance contracts contribute to the overall value of noncurrent assets.

8. **Long-term Investments**:
   - Include **Equity Securities**: Equity securities represent a type of long-term investment, indicating ownership in companies.

9. **Long-Term Investments**:
   - Can include **Equity-Method Investments**: These investments are part of the broader category of financial assets.

10. **Weighted-average Assumptions**:
    - Encompass the **Rate of Compensation Increase**: This rate is crucial for determining future benefit obligations.

11. **Service Cost**:

12. **Terms Valuation Recognition and Presentation**:
    - Includes **Total Shareholder Return Units (TSRUs)**: This program allocates TSRUs to reward key management and select employees.

13. **Reconciling Items**:

The relationships outlined involve the impact of foreign currency translation adjustments on various liability categories in financial reporting. 

1. **Other Current Liabilities** and **Adjustment**: Foreign currency translation adjustments can influence the reported amounts of other current liabilities, reflecting fluctuations in currency values.

2. **Adjustment** and **Other Current Liabilities**: Similarly, adjustments related to foreign currency translation can affect the reported figures for other current liabilities due to changes in currency values.

3. **Adjustment** and **Other Noncurrent Liabilities**: Foreign currency translation adjustments also play a role in affecting the reported amounts of other noncurrent liabilities, akin to their impact on current liabilities.

4. **Other Noncurrent Liabilities** and **Adjustment**: Just as with current liabilities, adjustments from foreign currency translation can impact the reported amounts of other noncurrent liabilities.

In summary, both current and noncurrent liabilities are subject to adjustments from foreign currency translations, which can significantly alter their reported values in financial statements.

The COVID-19 pandemic has had profound effects on various sectors, particularly consumer behavior and clinical trials. 

1. **Consumer Behavior**: The pandemic has significantly transformed how consumers engage with the market, notably increasing the prevalence of online shopping for medical treatments. This shift encompasses both legitimate products and counterfeit options, highlighting a critical change in consumer habits during this crisis.

2. **Clinical Trials**: The pandemic has severely impacted clinical trials, creating substantial challenges in recruiting participants and accumulating necessary case data. These disruptions have hindered the ability to conduct essential research and evaluate medical interventions effectively.

In summary, the COVID-19 pandemic has not only altered consumer behavior towards online medical shopping but has also disrupted the clinical trial landscape, complicating research efforts and participant engagement.

community_summary.618:
The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph primarily involve the role of The Bank of New York Mellon as the trustee for various supplemental indentures and its responsibilities towards the holders of these financial agreements. 

1. **Trustee and Indentures**: The Bank of New York Mellon serves as the trustee for multiple supplemental indentures, including the First, Second, Fourth, and Fifth Supplemental Indentures. In this capacity, the bank oversees the execution and compliance of the terms and conditions specified in each indenture, ensuring that the agreements are properly managed and adhered to.

2. **Trustee's Role in Securities Act**: The trustee's role is also contextualized within the Securities Act, which outlines regulations regarding beneficial ownership interests. This indicates that the trustee's responsibilities are influenced by legal frameworks that govern financial transactions and ownership rights.

3. **Representation of Holders**: The Bank of New York Mellon represents the interests of the holders of the indentures, particularly in situations of default. This representation involves acting on behalf of the holders to enforce the terms of the indenture, highlighting the trustee's critical role in protecting the rights of investors.

4. **Obligations to Holders**: While the trustee is responsible for managing the rights and powers under the indenture, it is only obligated to act upon the request of the holders, provided they offer reasonable indemnity. This stipulation emphasizes the conditional nature of the trustee's obligations, reinforcing the need for collaboration between the trustee and the holders.

In summary, The Bank of New York Mellon plays a pivotal role as the trustee for various supplemental indentures, ensuring compliance with financial agreements, representing the interests of holders, and operating within the regulatory framework established by the Securities Act.

3. **Financial Assets and Shares**: The relationship between financial assets and shares is highlighted through the concept of impairment charges, indicating that the valuation of shares can also be influenced by the overall assessment of financial assets.

1. **Deferred Tax Liabilities and Definite-lived Intangible Assets**: Deferred tax liabilities are closely associated with definite-lived intangible assets. This relationship is significant because the amortization of these assets can create temporary differences that lead to the recognition of deferred tax liabilities.

2. **Deferred Tax Liabilities and Outside Basis Differences**: Deferred tax liabilities are influenced by outside basis differences. This indicates that temporary differences, including those arising from outside basis differences, play a crucial role in the recognition of deferred tax liabilities.

3. **Insurance Coverage and Market Conditions**: Insurance coverage is influenced by market conditions, which directly affect its cost and availability. This relationship underscores the importance of external economic factors in determining the nature and extent of insurance coverage.

The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight key financial valuation practices involving various entities:

1. **Long-Term Receivables** and **Fair Value Measurements**: Long-term receivables are assessed within fair value measurements, utilizing Level 3 inputs, which are unobservable and critical for estimating their value.

2. **Commingled Funds** and **Partnership Investments**: Both commingled funds and partnership investments share a valuation approach based on year-end reported Net Asset Value (NAV) or its equivalent, reflecting a standardized method in financial reporting.

3. **Valuation Basis**: This serves as a central entity linking long-term receivables, commingled funds, and partnership investments, emphasizing the importance of fair value measurements and NAV in determining the value of these financial instruments.

Overall, the summary underscores the reliance on specific valuation methodologies—Level 3 inputs for long-term receivables and NAV for commingled funds and partnership investments—highlighting their significance in financial assessments.

1. **Manufactured By**: Lipitor, a medication primarily used to lower cholesterol, is manufactured by **Pfizer**, which oversees its development and marketing.

2. **Involved In Litigation**: **Ranbaxy Laboratories Ltd.** is implicated in class action lawsuits concerning Lipitor, indicating its involvement in legal disputes related to the drug.

3. **Brand-Generic Relationship**: Lipitor serves as the brand name for atorvastatin, while **Generic Lipitor** represents its non-branded counterpart, establishing a direct relationship between the two as brand and generic versions of the same medication.

4. **Generic Version Produced By**: **Ranbaxy** also produces a generic version of Lipitor, which is significant in the context of allegations regarding price overcharges associated with the drug.

In summary, Lipitor is a Pfizer-manufactured drug that faces legal scrutiny involving Ranbaxy, which not only produces a generic version but is also linked to litigation concerning the brand. The relationship between Lipitor and its generic counterpart underscores the complexities of pharmaceutical branding and pricing.

The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight various regulatory frameworks and their implications across different domains:

1. **WTO-TRIPS and Intellectual Property**: The WTO-TRIPS agreement establishes a global regulatory framework that sets standards and protections for intellectual property, influencing how intellectual property rights are managed worldwide.

2. **Tax Jurisdiction and Tax Treaties**: Tax treaties are created to regulate tax obligations within and between jurisdictions, with the primary goal of minimizing double taxation, thereby facilitating international trade and investment.

3. **IRC and Qualified Plans**: The Internal Revenue Code (IRC) defines the criteria for what constitutes a qualified retirement plan, which encompasses both Pension Plans and Defined Contribution Plans, ensuring compliance with federal regulations.

4. **GAAP and Pension Plans**: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) provide the accounting standards that govern the reporting of pension plans in financial statements, promoting consistency and transparency in financial reporting.

5. **Antitrust Laws and Unfair Competition**: Antitrust laws are designed to prevent unfair competition practices, ensuring a fair marketplace by prohibiting actions that could harm competition.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the interconnectedness of regulatory frameworks across intellectual property, taxation, retirement planning, financial reporting, and market competition, emphasizing their roles in establishing standards and promoting fairness in various sectors.

2. **International Swaps and Derivatives Association (ISDA)**: The ISDA master agreements, established by the International Swaps and Derivatives Association, provide a standardized framework for derivatives transactions, ensuring consistency and clarity in the financial markets.

3. **Delaware General Corporation Law and Corporate Bylaws**: The Bylaws of a corporation are governed by the Delaware General Corporation Law, which outlines the legal framework for corporate governance in Delaware, ensuring compliance and structure in corporate operations.

4. **Delaware Law and Board of Directors**: Delaware law governs the operations of the Board of Directors, including the authority to fill vacancies and manage corporate governance, thereby influencing corporate decision-making processes.

5. **Base Indenture and Notes**: The base indenture governs the terms and conditions under which financial notes are issued, establishing a legal relationship that defines the rights and obligations of the involved parties.

These relationships illustrate the critical frameworks that guide corporate governance, financial transactions, and collaborative agreements in various sectors.

The relationships between the entities Solutia and Pharmacia highlight significant legal and financial interactions primarily stemming from liabilities associated with Former Monsanto’s chemical businesses. 

1. **Indemnification**: Solutia has agreed to indemnify Pharmacia for liabilities related to Former Monsanto’s chemical businesses. This establishes a financial and legal responsibility where Solutia protects Pharmacia from certain claims.

2. **Defense in Litigation**: Solutia is actively defending Pharmacia in various claims and litigation that arise from the aforementioned chemical businesses. This indicates a commitment from Solutia to support Pharmacia in legal matters.

3. **Reorganization under Bankruptcy**: Solutia underwent a reorganization process under Chapter 11 of the U.S. Bankruptcy Code. This restructuring allowed Solutia to manage its debts and obligations effectively, which is crucial for its financial stability and ongoing operations.

Overall, the relationships underscore Solutia's role in providing legal protection and financial indemnity to Pharmacia while navigating its own financial restructuring.

The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight various entitlements associated with different financial instruments and agreements, primarily involving common stock and performance-based awards. Here’s a summary of the key entities and their relationships:

1. **Entitlement and Common Stock**:
   - **Restricted Stock Units (RSUs)**: Holders are entitled to receive a specified number of shares of common stock.
   - **Breakthrough Performance Awards**: These awards entitle holders to receive shares of common stock at the end of a performance period, linking the awards to financial compensation.
   - **Portfolio Performance Shares**: Employees are entitled to receive these shares based on performance metrics, which also result in common stock allocation.

2. **Entitlement and Corporate Governance**:
   - **Holders of Shares of Common Stock**: Shareholders are entitled to receive dividends as declared by the Board of Directors, establishing a direct relationship between shareholders and the governing body responsible for financial decisions.

3. **Entitlement and Financial Instruments**:
   - **Applicable Redemption Notes**: Registered holders are entitled to receive interest payments on these notes as specified in the indenture.

4. **Entitlement and Legal Agreements**:
   - **NYU and Pfizer**: NYU claims entitlement to royalties on Pfizer's sales of Xalkori under a Research and License Agreement, indicating a legal and financial relationship between the two entities.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the various ways in which entitlements are structured around stock ownership, performance-based compensation, and legal agreements, emphasizing the interconnectedness of financial rights and corporate governance.

The relationships summarized below highlight the key entities and their interconnections, primarily focusing on the COVID-19 vaccine Comirnaty and its associated treatment Paxlovid, both developed by Pfizer.

1. **Comirnaty and Paxlovid**: Both are developed by Pfizer to combat COVID-19, with Comirnaty functioning as a preventive vaccine and Paxlovid as a treatment for those infected. They are interconnected in their roles in managing the pandemic.

2. **Comirnaty and BNT162b2**: Comirnaty is the commercial name for the vaccine BNT162b2, indicating they are the same product referred to in different contexts.

3. **Emergency Use Authorization (EUA)**: Comirnaty is authorized for emergency use under the EUA, facilitating its distribution during public health emergencies.

4. **Supply Agreements**: Comirnaty is supplied to multiple countries through signed agreements, emphasizing its global distribution efforts.

5. **Financial Performance**: The revenues for Comirnaty in emerging markets have decreased, indicating challenges in financial performance in those regions. However, the total contracted revenue expected from Comirnaty is projected at $34.4 billion, reflecting significant financial commitments.

6. **Biopharmaceutical Context**: Comirnaty is categorized as a product within the biopharma industry, contributing to the sector's revenue.

7. **Revenue Recognition and Financial Year**: The financial year 2021 is significant for Comirnaty as it marks the recognition of deferred revenues, highlighting the vaccine's sales activities during that period. The revenues are also reflected in the cost of sales, indicating a direct financial relationship.

In summary, Comirnaty and Paxlovid are pivotal in the fight against COVID-19, with Comirnaty being a key biopharmaceutical product that has substantial financial implications and global distribution agreements, while also facing challenges in certain markets.
